content.text:
You are an expert cardiac electrophysiology and quantitative pharmacology assistant.

Your task is to write ONLY the LaTeX text for the Results section of a manuscript, describing simulation results for quinidine.

In THIS response, you must ONLY describe the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 1–4. Do NOT mention any figures beyond Figure 4. Every Figures need to be explained in one paragraph.

Structure:
- First, write 2–3 introductory Result paragraphs summarizing quantitative findings from the CSVs, without mentioning any Figures.
- Then write 4 comprehensive paragraphs, one for each of Figures 1–4, in order.

Fixed meanings:
- Figure 1: membrane potential over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-vm}.
- Figure 2: rate of change of membrane potential over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-dvmdt}.
- Figure 3: intracellular calcium concentration over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-cai}.
- Figure 4: late sodium current over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-inal}.

STRICT LATEX RULES:
- Output MUST be valid LaTeX body content only (no \documentclass, no preamble, no \begin{document}).
- Begin with \\section*{Methods}. Do not make any new section or subsection.
- Do NOT use Markdown at all: no backticks, no ``` fences, no # headings.
- Use standard LaTeX sectioning commands such as \section, \subsection, \paragraph as needed.
- You may use math mode with $...$ or \( ... \), but do NOT wrap the entire answer in a single math environment.
- Use ASCII characters only; avoid Unicode symbols.
- Make sure all braces { } and math modes are balanced.

Please generate the LaTeX text for the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 1–4 only, using the attached CSV time-series data, features summary, and LaTeX template as guidance. Follow the style and level of detail implied by the template.
